title: rubus fruticosus subsp. rudis
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. rudis, also known as wild raspberry, is a subspecies of bramble that belongs to the rose family and is characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruit. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of anthocyanins and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. rudis include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
